notes: rule.core.variant spans both deterministic gates: count/distinctness at layer 1 (LINT.VARIANT_COUNT, LINT.VARIANT_DUP via engine/gate/pre-gate-lint.js over draft inputs) and presence at the publish edge (PKG.RECOMMENDED_MISSING / PKG.VARIANT_A_MISSING / PKG.VARIANT_B_MISSING via engine/gate/validate-package.js over a package input). The presence positives apply an 'omit' to base.package.json (mirroring the live validate-package test's field deletion) so the on-disk base stays schema-valid; the schema-validation CI job validates base.package.json, not the mutated forms. LINT.VARIANT_COUNT's positive sets rules.variant_count:4 against a 3-variant (schema-valid) draft rather than shipping an off-count draft that would fail draft.schema.json's minItems:3.

cases.name: variant-count
cases.notes: Expected count 4, draft carries 3 — deterministic, hard. (Production default is N=3; the override drives a count mismatch from a schema-valid draft.)

cases.name: variant-dup
cases.notes: Recommended and Variant A are identical (one pairwise dup) — deterministic, hard.

cases.name: recommended-missing
cases.notes: Package with no Recommended variant — deterministic publish-edge, hard.

cases.name: clean-draft
cases.notes: Three distinct variants, default count — no LINT.VARIANT_* code.

cases.name: clean-package
cases.notes: Complete package with all three variants present — no PKG.*_MISSING code.
